#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Test all reasonable interpretations of applying roll/pitch Euler angles
to the gravity vector [0,0,-1] and find which interpretation produces
physically correct impact directions for the 26 cuboid drop orientations.

The cuboid coordinate system assumption:
  X = right/left
  Y = up/down \(top/bottom\)
  Z = back/front

Impact direction = the direction gravity points in the BODY frame,
i.e., which face/edge/corner of the cuboid hits the ground.

We rotate the world gravity vector into the body frame using various
Euler angle conventions and check which convention matches the expected
physical impact directions.
"""

import numpy as np
from itertools import product

# ============================================================
# Define the 26 drop orientations: \(name, roll_deg, pitch_deg\)
# ============================================================
ANGLES = {
    # === FACES \(6\) ===
    "F1_Back":    \(0, 0\),
    "F2_Front":   \(180, 0\),
    "F3_Right":   \(0, -90\),
    "F4_Left":    \(0, 90\),
    "F5_Top":     \(90, 0\),
    "F6_Bottom":  \(-90, 0\),
    # === EDGES \(12\) ===
    "E01_Back_Right":    \(0, -45\),
    "E02_Back_Left":     \(0, 45\),
    "E03_Back_Top":      \(45, 0\),
    "E04_Back_Bottom":   \(-45, 0\),
    "E05_Front_Right":   \(180, 45\),
    "E06_Front_Left":    \(180, -45\),
    "E07_Front_Top":     \(135, 0\),
    "E08_Front_Bottom":  \(-135, 0\),
    "E09_Right_Top":     \(90, -45\),
    "E10_Right_Bottom":  \(-90, -45\),
    "E11_Left_Top":      \(90, 45\),
    "E12_Left_Bottom":   \(-90, 45\),
    # === CORNERS \(8\) ===
    "C1_Back_Right_Top":     \(45, -45\),
    "C2_Back_Right_Bottom":  \(-45, -45\),
    "C3_Back_Left_Top":      \(45, 45\),
    "C4_Back_Left_Bottom":   \(-45, 45\),
    "C5_Front_Right_Top":    \(135, 45\),
    "C6_Front_Right_Bottom": \(-135, 45\),
    "C7_Front_Left_Top":     \(135, -45\),
    "C8_Front_Left_Bottom":  \(-135, -45\),
}

# ============================================================
# Expected impact directions \(unit vectors in body frame\)
# "Which direction does gravity point in the body frame?"
# i.e., which part of the cuboid hits the ground
# ============================================================
s2 = 1.0 / np.sqrt\(2\)
s3 = 1.0 / np.sqrt\(3\)

EXPECTED = {
    # Faces: axis-aligned
    "F1_Back":    np.array\([0, 0, -1]\),    # back face hits ground -> gravity in -Z body
    "F2_Front":   np.array\([0, 0,  1]\),    # front face hits ground -> gravity in +Z body
    "F3_Right":   np.array\([1, 0,  0]\),    # right side hits ground -> gravity in +X body
    "F4_Left":    np.array\([-1, 0, 0]\),    # left side hits ground -> gravity in -X body
    "F5_Top":     np.array\([0, 1,  0]\),    # top hits ground -> gravity in +Y body
    "F6_Bottom":  np.array\([0, -1, 0]\),    # bottom hits ground -> gravity in -Y body
    # Edges: two-component normalized vectors
    "E01_Back_Right":    np.array\([ s2, 0, -s2]\),  # back-right edge
    "E02_Back_Left":     np.array\([-s2, 0, -s2]\),  # back-left edge
    "E03_Back_Top":      np.array\([ 0,  s2, -s2]\), # back-top edge
    "E04_Back_Bottom":   np.array\([ 0, -s2, -s2]\), # back-bottom edge
    "E05_Front_Right":   np.array\([ s2, 0,  s2]\),  # front-right edge
    "E06_Front_Left":    np.array\([-s2, 0,  s2]\),  # front-left edge
    "E07_Front_Top":     np.array\([ 0,  s2,  s2]\), # front-top edge
    "E08_Front_Bottom":  np.array\([ 0, -s2,  s2]\), # front-bottom edge
    "E09_Right_Top":     np.array\([ s2,  s2, 0]\),  # right-top edge
    "E10_Right_Bottom":  np.array\([ s2, -s2, 0]\),  # right-bottom edge
    "E11_Left_Top":      np.array\([-s2,  s2, 0]\),  # left-top edge
    "E12_Left_Bottom":   np.array\([-s2, -s2, 0]\),  # left-bottom edge
    # Corners: three-component normalized vectors
    "C1_Back_Right_Top":     np.array\([ s3,  s3, -s3]\),
    "C2_Back_Right_Bottom":  np.array\([ s3, -s3, -s3]\),
    "C3_Back_Left_Top":      np.array\([-s3,  s3, -s3]\),
    "C4_Back_Left_Bottom":   np.array\([-s3, -s3, -s3]\),
    "C5_Front_Right_Top":    np.array\([ s3,  s3,  s3]\),
    "C6_Front_Right_Bottom": np.array\([ s3, -s3,  s3]\),
    "C7_Front_Left_Top":     np.array\([-s3,  s3,  s3]\),
    "C8_Front_Left_Bottom":  np.array\([-s3, -s3,  s3]\),
}


def Rx\(angle_deg\):
    """Rotation matrix about X axis."""
    a = np.radians\(angle_deg\)
    c, s = np.cos\(a\), np.sin\(a\)
    return np.array\([
        [1,  0,  0],
        [0,  c, -s],
        [0,  s,  c],
    ]\)

def Ry\(angle_deg\):
    """Rotation matrix about Y axis."""
    a = np.radians\(angle_deg\)
    c, s = np.cos\(a\), np.sin\(a\)
    return np.array\([
        [ c, 0, s],
        [ 0, 1, 0],
        [-s, 0, c],
    ]\)

def Rz\(angle_deg\):
    """Rotation matrix about Z axis."""
    a = np.radians\(angle_deg\)
    c, s = np.cos\(a\), np.sin\(a\)
    return np.array\([
        [c, -s, 0],
        [s,  c, 0],
        [0,  0, 1],
    ]\)


def check_interpretation\(name, compute_func, verbose=False\):
    """
    Test an interpretation against all 26 expected directions.

    compute_func\(roll_deg, pitch_deg\) -> 3D unit vector \(impact direction\)

    Returns \(n_correct, details_list\).
    """
    n_correct = 0
    details = []
    for label, \(roll, pitch\) in ANGLES.items\(\):
        v = compute_func\(roll, pitch\)
        # Normalize for safety
        norm = np.linalg.norm\(v\)
        if norm < 1e-10:
            v = np.array\([0, 0, 0]\)
        else:
            v = v / norm

        expected = EXPECTED[label]
        match = np.allclose\(v, expected, atol=1e-6\)
        if match:
            n_correct += 1

        details.append\(\(label, roll, pitch, v, expected, match\)\)

    return n_correct, details


def print_results\(name, n_correct, details, show_all=False\):
    """Print results for one interpretation."""
    total = len\(details\)

    # Count by category
    face_ok = sum\(1 for d in details if d[0].startswith\("F"\) and d[5]\)
    edge_ok = sum\(1 for d in details if d[0].startswith\("E"\) and d[5]\)
    corner_ok = sum\(1 for d in details if d[0].startswith\("C"\) and d[5]\)

    marker = " <<<< PERFECT" if n_correct == total else ""
    print\(f"\\n{'='*80}"\)
    print\(f"  {name}"\)
    print\(f"  Score: {n_correct}/{total}  \(Faces: {face_ok}/6, Edges: {edge_ok}/12, Corners: {corner_ok}/8\){marker}"\)
    print\(f"{'='*80}"\)

    if show_all or n_correct < total:
        for label, roll, pitch, got, expected, match in details:
            status = "OK " if match else "BAD"
            got_str = f"\({got[0]:+7.4f}, {got[1]:+7.4f}, {got[2]:+7.4f}\)"
            exp_str = f"\({expected[0]:+7.4f}, {expected[1]:+7.4f}, {expected[2]:+7.4f}\)"
            if match and not show_all:
                continue
            print\(f"  [{status}] {label:28s} roll={roll:+5.0f} pitch={pitch:+5.0f}  "
                  f"got={got_str}  expected={exp_str}"\)

    return n_correct


def main\(\):
    print\("=" * 80\)
    print\(" EULER ANGLE INTERPRETATION TESTER FOR 26 CUBOID DROP ORIENTATIONS"\)
    print\("=" * 80\)
    print\(\)
    print\("Coordinate system: X=right, Y=up, Z=back"\)
    print\("Gravity in world frame: tested with multiple initial vectors"\)
    print\("We seek the rotation R such that R * g_initial gives the correct"\)
    print\("impact direction in the body frame for each \(roll, pitch\) pair."\)
    print\(\)
    print\("Expected: F1_Back\(0,0\) -> \(0,0,-1\), F3_Right\(0,-90\) -> \(1,0,0\), etc."\)
    print\(\)

    # Initial gravity vectors to try
    gravity_vectors = {
        "-Z": np.array\([0, 0, -1]\),
        "+Z": np.array\([0, 0,  1]\),
        "-Y": np.array\([0, -1, 0]\),
        "+Y": np.array\([0,  1, 0]\),
        "-X": np.array\([-1, 0, 0]\),
        "+X": np.array\([ 1, 0, 0]\),
    }

    # Axis assignment possibilities for roll and pitch
    axis_combos = {
        "roll=Rx, pitch=Ry": \(Rx, Ry\),
        "roll=Rx, pitch=Rz": \(Rx, Rz\),
        "roll=Ry, pitch=Rx": \(Ry, Rx\),
        "roll=Ry, pitch=Rz": \(Ry, Rz\),
        "roll=Rz, pitch=Rx": \(Rz, Rx\),
        "roll=Rz, pitch=Ry": \(Rz, Ry\),
    }

    # Sign combos for roll and pitch
    sign_combos = [
        \("+roll", "+pitch", 1, 1\),
        \("-roll", "+pitch", -1, 1\),
        \("+roll", "-pitch", 1, -1\),
        \("-roll", "-pitch", -1, -1\),
    ]

    # Order combos:
    # "roll_first"  => R = R_pitch * R_roll \(roll applied first to vector\)
    # "pitch_first" => R = R_roll * R_pitch \(pitch applied first to vector\)
    order_combos = [
        \("roll_first",  lambda R_r, R_p: R_p @ R_r\),   # v = R_pitch * R_roll * g
        \("pitch_first", lambda R_r, R_p: R_r @ R_p\),   # v = R_roll * R_pitch * g
    ]

    results = []

    total_combos = \(len\(gravity_vectors\) * len\(axis_combos\) * len\(sign_combos\)
                    * len\(order_combos\) * 2\)  # *2 for transpose
    print\(f"Testing {total_combos} total interpretations...\\n"\)

    for grav_name, g_vec in gravity_vectors.items\(\):
        for axis_name, \(R_roll_fn, R_pitch_fn\) in axis_combos.items\(\):
            for sign_name_r, sign_name_p, sr, sp in sign_combos:
                for order_name, combine_fn in order_combos:

                    # Forward rotation: R * g
                    interp_name = \(f"g={grav_name}, {axis_name}, "
                                   f"{sign_name_r},{sign_name_p}, {order_name}"\)

                    def compute\(roll, pitch, _sr=sr, _sp=sp, _g=g_vec,
                                _R_r=R_roll_fn, _R_p=R_pitch_fn, _combine=combine_fn\):
                        R_r = _R_r\(_sr * roll\)
                        R_p = _R_p\(_sp * pitch\)
                        R = _combine\(R_r, R_p\)
                        return R @ _g

                    n_correct, details = check_interpretation\(interp_name, compute\)
                    results.append\(\(n_correct, interp_name, details\)\)

                    # Transpose \(inverse\) rotation: R^T * g
                    interp_name_t = interp_name + ", TRANSPOSE"

                    def compute_t\(roll, pitch, _sr=sr, _sp=sp, _g=g_vec,
                                  _R_r=R_roll_fn, _R_p=R_pitch_fn, _combine=combine_fn\):
                        R_r = _R_r\(_sr * roll\)
                        R_p = _R_p\(_sp * pitch\)
                        R = _combine\(R_r, R_p\)
                        return R.T @ _g

                    n_correct_t, details_t = check_interpretation\(interp_name_t, compute_t\)
                    results.append\(\(n_correct_t, interp_name_t, details_t\)\)

    # Sort by score descending
    results.sort\(key=lambda x: -x[0]\)

    # Print summary of top results
    print\("\\n" + "=" * 80\)
    print\(" SUMMARY: TOP 30 INTERPRETATIONS \(sorted by score\)"\)
    print\("=" * 80\)

    for i, \(score, name, _\) in enumerate\(results[:30]\):
        marker = " <<<< PERFECT!" if score == 26 else ""
        print\(f"  {i+1:3d}. Score {score:2d}/26  {name}{marker}"\)

    # Print detailed results for the best interpretation\(s\)
    best_score = results[0][0]
    print\(f"\\n\\n{'#'*80}"\)
    print\(f" DETAILED RESULTS FOR BEST INTERPRETATIONS \(score = {best_score}/26\)"\)
    print\(f"{'#'*80}"\)

    count = 0
    for score, name, details in results:
        if score == best_score and count < 5:
            print_results\(name, score, details, show_all=True\)
            count += 1

    # If no perfect match, also show some near-misses for analysis
    if best_score < 26:
        print\(f"\\n\\n{'#'*80}"\)
        print\(f" ANALYSIS OF NEAR-MISS FAILURES"\)
        print\(f"{'#'*80}"\)

        # Show the first best interpretation with only failed cases
        best_name = results[0][1]
        best_details = results[0][2]
        print\(f"\\n  Interpretation: {best_name}"\)
        print\(f"  Failures only:"\)
        for label, roll, pitch, got, expected, match in best_details:
            if not match:
                got_str = f"\({got[0]:+7.4f}, {got[1]:+7.4f}, {got[2]:+7.4f}\)"
                exp_str = f"\({expected[0]:+7.4f}, {expected[1]:+7.4f}, {expected[2]:+7.4f}\)"
                # Check if it matches the negation
                neg_match = np.allclose\(got, -expected, atol=1e-6\)
                swap_info = " \(negated match!\)" if neg_match else ""
                print\(f"    {label:28s} roll={roll:+5.0f} pitch={pitch:+5.0f}  "
                      f"got={got_str}  expected={exp_str}{swap_info}"\)

    # ============================================================
    # Specific formula tests
    # ============================================================
    print\(f"\\n\\n{'#'*80}"\)
    print\(f" SPECIFIC FORMULA TESTS"\)
    print\(f"{'#'*80}"\)

    specific_tests = [
        \("v = Rx\(roll\) * Ry\(pitch\) * [0,0,-1]",
         lambda r, p: Rx\(r\) @ Ry\(p\) @ np.array\([0, 0, -1]\)\),
        \("v = Ry\(pitch\) * Rx\(roll\) * [0,0,-1]",
         lambda r, p: Ry\(p\) @ Rx\(r\) @ np.array\([0, 0, -1]\)\),
        \("v = Rx\(roll\) * Ry\(-pitch\) * [0,0,-1]",
         lambda r, p: Rx\(r\) @ Ry\(-p\) @ np.array\([0, 0, -1]\)\),
        \("v = Ry\(-pitch\) * Rx\(roll\) * [0,0,-1]",
         lambda r, p: Ry\(-p\) @ Rx\(r\) @ np.array\([0, 0, -1]\)\),
        \("v = Rx\(-roll\) * Ry\(pitch\) * [0,0,-1]",
         lambda r, p: Rx\(-r\) @ Ry\(p\) @ np.array\([0, 0, -1]\)\),
        \("v = Rx\(-roll\) * Ry\(-pitch\) * [0,0,-1]",
         lambda r, p: Rx\(-r\) @ Ry\(-p\) @ np.array\([0, 0, -1]\)\),
    ]

    for name, func in specific_tests:
        n, d = check_interpretation\(name, func\)
        print_results\(name, n, d, show_all=\(n >= best_score - 2\)\)

    # ============================================================
    # Distribution check for the best interpretation
    # ============================================================
    if best_score >= 20:
        print\(f"\\n\\n{'#'*80}"\)
        print\(f" SPHERICAL DISTRIBUTION CHECK FOR BEST INTERPRETATION"\)
        print\(f"{'#'*80}"\)

        best_details = results[0][2]
        vectors = [d[3] for d in best_details]

        # Check all vectors are unit vectors
        all_unit = all\(abs\(np.linalg.norm\(v\) - 1.0\) < 1e-6 for v in vectors\)
        print\(f"\\n  All unit vectors: {all_unit}"\)

        # Check symmetry: for each vector v, -v should also be present
        n_antipodal = 0
        for v in vectors:
            has_antipodal = any\(np.allclose\(v, -w, atol=1e-6\) for w in vectors\)
            if has_antipodal:
                n_antipodal += 1
        print\(f"  Antipodal pairs: {n_antipodal}/26 vectors have their -v counterpart"\)

        # Check the magnitudes of components
        faces = [d for d in best_details if d[0].startswith\("F"\)]
        edges = [d for d in best_details if d[0].startswith\("E"\)]
        corners = [d for d in best_details if d[0].startswith\("C"\)]

        print\(f"\\n  Face vectors \(should have exactly 1 nonzero component\):"\)
        for d in faces:
            v = d[3]
            n_nonzero = np.sum\(np.abs\(v\) > 1e-6\)
            status = "OK" if n_nonzero == 1 else "BAD"
            print\(f"    [{status}] {d[0]:28s}  \({v[0]:+7.4f}, {v[1]:+7.4f}, {v[2]:+7.4f}\)  "
                  f"nonzero={n_nonzero}"\)

        print\(f"\\n  Edge vectors \(should have exactly 2 nonzero components\):"\)
        for d in edges:
            v = d[3]
            n_nonzero = np.sum\(np.abs\(v\) > 1e-6\)
            status = "OK" if n_nonzero == 2 else "BAD"
            print\(f"    [{status}] {d[0]:28s}  \({v[0]:+7.4f}, {v[1]:+7.4f}, {v[2]:+7.4f}\)  "
                  f"nonzero={n_nonzero}"\)

        print\(f"\\n  Corner vectors \(should have exactly 3 nonzero components\):"\)
        for d in corners:
            v = d[3]
            n_nonzero = np.sum\(np.abs\(v\) > 1e-6\)
            status = "OK" if n_nonzero == 3 else "BAD"
            print\(f"    [{status}] {d[0]:28s}  \({v[0]:+7.4f}, {v[1]:+7.4f}, {v[2]:+7.4f}\)  "
                  f"nonzero={n_nonzero}"\)

        # Check uniqueness
        unique_count = 0
        for i, v in enumerate\(vectors\):
            is_dup = False
            for j in range\(i\):
                if np.allclose\(v, vectors[j], atol=1e-6\):
                    is_dup = True
                    break
            if not is_dup:
                unique_count += 1
        print\(f"\\n  Unique direction vectors: {unique_count}/26"\)

    # ============================================================
    # Final conclusion
    # ============================================================
    print\(f"\\n\\n{'='*80}"\)
    print\(f" CONCLUSION"\)
    print\(f"{'='*80}"\)

    if best_score == 26:
        perfect = [\(s, n, d\) for s, n, d in results if s == 26]
        print\(f"\\n  Found {len\(perfect\)} perfect interpretation\(s\):"\)
        for _, name, _ in perfect:
            print\(f"    - {name}"\)
        print\(\)
        print\("  The rotation formula for computing the gravity/impact direction"\)
        print\("  in the body frame from \(roll, pitch\) angles is identified above."\)
    else:
        print\(f"\\n  No perfect interpretation found. Best score: {best_score}/26."\)
        print\(\)

        # Analyze pattern of failures
        best_details = results[0][2]
        face_fails = [\(d[0], d[3], d[4]\) for d in best_details
                      if d[0].startswith\("F"\) and not d[5]]
        edge_fails = [\(d[0], d[3], d[4]\) for d in best_details
                      if d[0].startswith\("E"\) and not d[5]]
        corner_fails = [\(d[0], d[3], d[4]\) for d in best_details
                        if d[0].startswith\("C"\) and not d[5]]

        print\(f"  Failure breakdown for best interpretation:"\)
        print\(f"    Face failures:   {len\(face_fails\)}/6"\)
        print\(f"    Edge failures:   {len\(edge_fails\)}/12"\)
        print\(f"    Corner failures: {len\(corner_fails\)}/8"\)

        if face_fails:
            print\(f"\\n  Face failures suggest the axis mapping or initial vector is wrong."\)
        elif edge_fails and not corner_fails:
            print\(f"\\n  Only edge failures - may be a sign convention issue on angles."\)
        elif corner_fails and not edge_fails:
            print\(f"\\n  Only corner failures - the 45-degree combined rotations"\)
            print\(f"  do not decompose cleanly in this convention."\)
        else:
            print\(f"\\n  Mixed failures in edges and corners suggest the rotation"\)
            print\(f"  order or angle convention needs adjustment."\)
            print\(f"\\n  Consider: the corner angles \(roll=+/-45, pitch=+/-45\) produce"\)
            print\(f"  vectors with unequal components because the two rotations"\)
            print\(f"  are not independent when composed. This is expected for"\)
            print\(f"  standard Euler angles - the 26-direction set may require"\)
            print\(f"  a non-standard approach \(e.g., Rodrigues rotation or"\)
            print\(f"  quaternion-based direction computation\)."\)

    print\(\)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main\(\)

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
PART 2: Understanding WHY no standard Euler angle convention produces
all 26 directions correctly, and what the CORRECT angles should be.

Key finding from Part 1:
  v = Rx\(roll\) * Ry\(pitch\) * [0,0,-1]
  gives 16/26 correct \(all faces, 10/12 edges, 0/8 corners\).

The problem: Euler angle rotations are NOT commutative. When we apply
pitch=45 after roll=45, the result is NOT the same as rotating 45
degrees in each axis independently. The second rotation acts on the
ALREADY-ROTATED axes.

For corners, we need pitch = arctan\(1/sqrt\(2\)\) = 35.264 degrees
\(not 45 degrees\) to get equal x,y,z components.

This script:
1. Derives the correct pitch angle for corners mathematically
2. Shows what the actual correct angles should be for all 26 directions
3. Tests both the "naive" 45-degree angles and the corrected angles
4. Identifies the two edge failures \(E05, E06\) and their fix
"""

import numpy as np


def Rx\(angle_deg\):
    a = np.radians\(angle_deg\)
    c, s = np.cos\(a\), np.sin\(a\)
    return np.array\([[1, 0, 0], [0, c, -s], [0, s, c]]\)

def Ry\(angle_deg\):
    a = np.radians\(angle_deg\)
    c, s = np.cos\(a\), np.sin\(a\)
    return np.array\([[c, 0, s], [0, 1, 0], [-s, 0, c]]\)

def Rz\(angle_deg\):
    a = np.radians\(angle_deg\)
    c, s = np.cos\(a\), np.sin\(a\)
    return np.array\([[c, -s, 0], [s, c, 0], [0, 0, 1]]\)


s2 = 1.0 / np.sqrt\(2\)
s3 = 1.0 / np.sqrt\(3\)

# The formula we found works best:
# impact_direction = Rx\(roll\) * Ry\(pitch\) * [0, 0, -1]
#
# Let's derive what pitch angle gives equal x,y,z for corners.
# With roll=45, pitch=p, and g=[0,0,-1]:
#
# v = Rx\(45\) * Ry\(p\) * [0,0,-1]
# Ry\(p\)*[0,0,-1] = [-sin\(p\), 0, -cos\(p\)]
# Rx\(45\)*[-sin\(p\), 0, -cos\(p\)] = [-sin\(p\), cos\(p\)/sqrt\(2\), -cos\(p\)/sqrt\(2\)]

physically correct impact directions for the 26 cuboid drop orientations.

=========

   This gives the closed-form: v = [-sin\(p\), sin\(r\)*cos\(p\), -cos\(r\)*cos\(p\)]

   - 0/8 corners: FAIL \(produces 0.707/0.500/0.500 instead of 0.577/0.577/0.577\)

    a = np.radians\(angle_deg\)

    return np.array\([[1, 0, 0], [0, c, -s], [0, s, c]]\)

    return np.array\([[c, 0, s], [0, 1, 0], [-s, 0, c]]\)

    print\("    2. Determine sign_y from roll:"\)
    print\("         0 < roll < 180  => +Y \(top\),  -180 < roll < 0 => -Y \(bottom\),  else 0"\)
    print\("    3. Determine sign_z from roll:"\)
    print\("         |roll| < 90 => -Z \(back\),  |roll| > 90 => +Z \(front\),  |roll|=90 => 0"\)
    print\("    4. Build vector \(sign_x, sign_y, sign_z\) and normalize."\)
    print\(\)

    def sign_decode\(roll, pitch\):
        """Decode roll/pitch as sign indicators, not rotation angles."""
        # X component from pitch
        if pitch < -1e-6:
            sx = 1   # right
        elif pitch > 1e-6:
            sx = -1  # left
        else:
            sx = 0

        # Y component from roll magnitude \(not sign of Z\)
        # roll in \(0, 180\) exclusive => top, roll in \(-180, 0\) exclusive => bottom
        if 1e-6 < roll < 180 - 1e-6:
            sy = 1   # top
        elif -180 + 1e-6 < roll < -1e-6:
            sy = -1  # bottom
        else:
            sy = 0

        # Z component from roll quadrant
        # |roll| < 90 => back \(-Z\), |roll| > 90 => front \(+Z\)
        abs_roll = abs\(roll\)
        if abs_roll < 90 - 1e-6:
            sz = -1  # back
        elif abs_roll > 90 + 1e-6:
            sz = 1   # front
        else:
            sz = 0   # neither \(pure right/left/top/bottom\)

        v = np.array\([float\(sx\), float\(sy\), float\(sz\)]\)
        norm = np.linalg.norm\(v\)
        return v / norm if norm > 1e-10 else v

    n_ok, details = check_formula\("Sign-decode approach", sign_decode\)
    print_details\("Sign-decode approach", n_ok, details, show_all=True\)

    \("C5_Front_Right_Top",    np.array\([ s3,  s3,  s3]\)\),
    \("C6_Front_Right_Bottom", np.array\([ s3, -s3,  s3]\)\),
    \("C7_Front_Left_Top",     np.array\([-s3,  s3,  s3]\)\),
    \("C8_Front_Left_Bottom",  np.array\([-s3, -s3,  s3]\)\),
]\)


def check_formula\(name, compute_func\):
    n_ok = 0
    details = []
    for label, \(roll, pitch\) in ANGLES.items\(\):
        v = compute_func\(roll, pitch\)
        norm = np.linalg.norm\(v\)
        v = v / norm if norm > 1e-10 else np.zeros\(3\)
        expected = EXPECTED[label]
        ok = np.allclose\(v, expected, atol=1e-6\)
        if ok:
            n_ok += 1
        details.append\(\(label, roll, pitch, v, expected, ok\)\)
    return n_ok, details


def print_details\(name, n_ok, details, show_all=True, failures_only=False\):
    total = len\(details\)
    f_ok = sum\(1 for d in details if d[0].startswith\("F"\) and d[5]\)
    e_ok = sum\(1 for d in details if d[0].startswith\("E"\) and d[5]\)
    c_ok = sum\(1 for d in details if d[0].startswith\("C"\) and d[5]\)
    tag = " <<<< PERFECT" if n_ok == total else ""

    print\(f"\\n{'='*90}"\)
    print\(f"  {name}"\)
    print\(f"  Score: {n_ok}/{total}  \(F:{f_ok}/6  E:{e_ok}/12  C:{c_ok}/8\){tag}"\)
    print\(f"{'='*90}"\)

    for label, roll, pitch, got, exp, ok in details:
        if failures_only and ok:
            continue
        if not show_all and not failures_only and ok:
            continue
        t = "OK " if ok else "BAD"
        g = f"\({got[0]:+7.4f}, {got[1]:+7.4f}, {got[2]:+7.4f}\)"
        e = f"\({exp[0]:+7.4f}, {exp[1]:+7.4f}, {exp[2]:+7.4f}\)"
        print\(f"  [{t}] {label:28s} r={roll:+5.0f} p={pitch:+5.0f}  got={g}  exp={e}"\)

#  PART 1: Exhaustive search
# ============================================================
def part1_exhaustive_search\(\):
    print\(\)
    print\("#" * 90\)
    print\("#  PART 1: EXHAUSTIVE SEARCH \(576 standard Euler interpretations\)"\)
    print\("#" * 90\)

    gravity_vectors = {
        "-Z": np.array\([0, 0, -1]\),  "+Z": np.array\([0, 0, 1]\),
        "-Y": np.array\([0, -1, 0]\),  "+Y": np.array\([0, 1, 0]\),
        "-X": np.array\([-1, 0, 0]\),  "+X": np.array\([1, 0, 0]\),
    }
    from functools import partial
    import types

    def Rz\(angle_deg\):
        a = np.radians\(angle_deg\)
        c, s = np.cos\(a\), np.sin\(a\)
        return np.array\([[c, -s, 0], [s, c, 0], [0, 0, 1]]\)

    axis_combos = {
        "Rx,Ry": \(Rx, Ry\), "Rx,Rz": \(Rx, Rz\), "Ry,Rx": \(Ry, Rx\),
        "Ry,Rz": \(Ry, Rz\), "Rz,Rx": \(Rz, Rx\), "Rz,Ry": \(Rz, Ry\),
    }

    results = []
    for gn, gv in gravity_vectors.items\(\):
        for an, \(Rr, Rp\) in axis_combos.items\(\):
            for sr, sp in [\(1,1\),\(-1,1\),\(1,-1\),\(-1,-1\)]:
                for order, combine in [\("r*p", lambda a,b: a@b\), \("p*r", lambda a,b: b@a\)]:
                    for tr_label, tr in [\("", False\), \("^T", True\)]:
                        def mk\(roll, pitch, _sr=sr, _sp=sp, _g=gv,
                               _Rr=Rr, _Rp=Rp, _c=combine, _t=tr\):
                            R = _c\(_Rr\(_sr*roll\), _Rp\(_sp*pitch\)\)
                            return \(R.T if _t else R\) @ _g
                        sn = f"{sr:+d}r,{sp:+d}p"
                        nm = f"g={gn} {an} {sn} {order}{tr_label}"
                        n, d = check_formula\(nm, mk\)
                        results.append\(\(n, nm, d\)\)

    results.sort\(key=lambda x: -x[0]\)
    print\(f"\\n  Tested {len\(results\)} interpretations.\\n"\)
    print\("  TOP 10:"\)
    for i, \(s, n, _\) in enumerate\(results[:10]\):
        tag = " <<<< PERFECT!" if s == 26 else ""
        print\(f"    {i+1:3d}. {s:2d}/26  {n}{tag}"\)

    best = results[0][0]
    if best < 26:
        print\(f"\\n  NO PERFECT MATCH. Best = {best}/26."\)
    return results

def part3_corrected_angles\(\):
    print\(\)
    print\("#" * 90\)

    print\("#" * 90\)
    print\(\)
    print\("  For v = Rx\(r\)*Ry\(p\)*[0,0,-1], inverse: p=arcsin\(-dx\), r=atan2\(dy,-dz\)"\)
    print\(\)

    g = np.array\([0, 0, -1]\)
    print\(f"  {'Name':28s} {'Orig':>13s} {'Correct':>14s} {'Note':12s}"\)

def part4_sign_decode\(\):
    print\(\)
    print\("#" * 90\)
    print\("#  PART 4: SIGN-DECODE APPROACHES"\)
    print\("#" * 90\)

    # Approach 4a: naive sign decode
    print\(\)
    print\("  --- 4a: Naive sign decode \(pitch sign -> X, roll sign -> Y, |roll| -> Z\) ---"\)

    def decode_naive\(roll, pitch\):
        sx = -1 if pitch > 1e-6 else \(1 if pitch < -1e-6 else 0\)
        sy = 1 if \(1e-6 < roll < 180-1e-6\) else \(-1 if \(-180+1e-6 < roll < -1e-6\) else 0\)
        abs_r = abs\(roll\)
        sz = -1 if abs_r < 90-1e-6 else \(1 if abs_r > 90+1e-6 else 0\)
        v = np.array\([float\(sx\), float\(sy\), float\(sz\)]\)
        n = np.linalg.norm\(v\)
        return v/n if n > 1e-10 else v

    n, d = check_formula\("4a: Naive sign decode", decode_naive\)
    print_details\("4a: Naive sign decode", n, d, show_all=False, failures_only=True\)

    # Approach 4b: Fixed sign decode -- account for pitch flip when |roll| > 90
    print\(\)
    print\("  --- 4b: Fixed sign decode \(negate pitch interpretation when |roll| > 90\) ---"\)
    print\("  When |roll| > 90, the object is 'upside down' in the Z sense."\)
    print\("  This means pitch=-45 with roll=180 should give LEFT, not RIGHT,"\)
    print\("  because the front-facing orientation reverses the pitch axis."\)

    def decode_fixed\(roll, pitch\):
        abs_r = abs\(roll\)
        # Z from roll quadrant
        sz = -1 if abs_r < 90-1e-6 else \(1 if abs_r > 90+1e-6 else 0\)

        # X from pitch, but flip if we're in the "front" hemisphere
        flip_x = -1 if abs_r > 90+1e-6 else 1
        sx = flip_x * \(-1 if pitch > 1e-6 else \(1 if pitch < -1e-6 else 0\)\)

        # Y from roll sign \(unaffected by Z direction\)
        sy = 1 if \(1e-6 < roll < 180-1e-6\) else \(-1 if \(-180+1e-6 < roll < -1e-6\) else 0\)

        v = np.array\([float\(sx\), float\(sy\), float\(sz\)]\)
        n = np.linalg.norm\(v\)
        return v/n if n > 1e-10 else v

    n, d = check_formula\("4b: Fixed sign decode \(flip X when front\)", decode_fixed\)
    print_details\("4b: Fixed sign decode \(flip X when front\)", n, d, show_all=True\)

    return n

def part5_lookup\(\):
    print\(\)
    print\("#" * 90\)
    print\("#  PART 5: DIRECT LOOKUP TABLE APPROACH"\)
    print\("#" * 90\)
    print\(\)
    print\("  Since the angles are really just identifiers, the most robust approach"\)
    print\("  is a direct mapping from the direction NAME to the vector."\)
    print\(\)
    print\("  def get_impact_direction\(name\):"\)
    print\("      # Parse name components"\)
    print\("      x = 1 if 'Right' in name else \(-1 if 'Left' in name else 0\)"\)
    print\("      y = 1 if 'Top' in name else \(-1 if 'Bottom' in name else 0\)"\)
    print\("      z = -1 if 'Back' in name else \(1 if 'Front' in name else 0\)"\)
    print\("      v = np.array\([x, y, z], dtype=float\)"\)
    print\("      return v / np.linalg.norm\(v\)"\)
    print\(\)

    def from_name\(name\):
        x = 1.0 if 'Right' in name else \(-1.0 if 'Left' in name else 0.0\)
        y = 1.0 if 'Top' in name else \(-1.0 if 'Bottom' in name else 0.0\)
        z = -1.0 if 'Back' in name else \(1.0 if 'Front' in name else 0.0\)
        v = np.array\([x, y, z]\)
        n = np.linalg.norm\(v\)
        return v/n if n > 1e-10 else v

    n_ok = 0
    for name, exp in EXPECTED.items\(\):
        v = from_name\(name\)
        ok = np.allclose\(v, exp, atol=1e-6\)
        if ok:
            n_ok += 1
        else:
            print\(f"  FAIL: {name}: got {v}, expected {exp}"\)

    print\(f"  Name-based lookup: {n_ok}/26 correct"\)
    if n_ok == 26:
        print\("  PERFECT SCORE!"\)

def part6_distribution\(\):
    print\(\)
    print\("#" * 90\)
    print\("#  PART 6: SPHERICAL DISTRIBUTION VERIFICATION"\)
    print\("#" * 90\)
    print\(\)

    vectors = list\(EXPECTED.values\(\)\)
    all_unit = all\(abs\(np.linalg.norm\(v\) - 1.0\) < 1e-6 for v in vectors\)
    n_anti = sum\(1 for v in vectors
                 if any\(np.allclose\(v, -w, atol=1e-6\) for w in vectors\)\)
    unique = []
    for v in vectors:
        if not any\(np.allclose\(v, u, atol=1e-6\) for u in unique\):
            unique.append\(v\)

    angles = []
    for i in range\(len\(vectors\)\):
        for j in range\(i+1, len\(vectors\)\):
            dot = np.clip\(np.dot\(vectors[i], vectors[j]\), -1, 1\)
            angles.append\(np.degrees\(np.arccos\(dot\)\)\)
    angles.sort\(\)

    print\(f"  All unit vectors:         {all_unit}"\)
    print\(f"  Antipodal partners:       {n_anti}/26"\)
    print\(f"  Unique directions:        {len\(unique\)}/26"\)
    print\(f"  Min angular separation:   {angles[0]:.1f} deg"\)
    print\(f"  Median angular sep:       {angles[len\(angles\)//2]:.1f} deg"\)
    print\(f"  Max angular sep:          {angles[-1]:.1f} deg"\)
    print\(\)

    for cat, prefix, nz in [\("Faces", "F", 1\), \("Edges", "E", 2\), \("Corners", "C", 3\)]:
        items = [\(n, v\) for n, v in EXPECTED.items\(\) if n.startswith\(prefix\)]
        ok = all\(np.sum\(np.abs\(v\) > 1e-6\) == nz for _, v in items\)
        print\(f"  {cat:8s}: all have {nz} nonzero component\(s\): {ok}"\)

def bonus_perfect_sign_decode\(\):
    """
    The 4b decode gets 24/26, failing only on F3_Right \(0,-90\) and F4_Left \(0,+90\).
    These fail because pitch=+/-90 with roll=0 produces Z=-1 \(back\) along with X,
    but the expected result is pure X with no Z.

    Fix: when |pitch| = 90, the Z component should be 0 \(pure right/left face\).
    More generally: if |pitch| = 90, sz = 0 regardless of roll.
    """
    print\(\)
    print\("#" * 90\)
    print\("#  BONUS: PERFECT SIGN-DECODE \(handles |pitch|=90 edge case\)"\)
    print\("#" * 90\)
    print\(\)
    print\("  Fix: when |pitch| = 90 deg, force sz = 0 \(pure right/left face\)."\)
    print\("  This is because a 90-degree pitch means the object is tilted fully"\)
    print\("  sideways -- the back/front distinction no longer applies."\)

    def decode_perfect\(roll, pitch\):
        abs_r = abs\(roll\)
        abs_p = abs\(pitch\)

        # Z from roll quadrant, but ONLY if |pitch| < 90
        if abs_p > 90 - 1e-6:
            sz = 0  # pure sideways tilt, no back/front
        else:
            sz = -1 if abs_r < 90 - 1e-6 else \(1 if abs_r > 90 + 1e-6 else 0\)

        sx = flip_x * \(-1 if pitch > 1e-6 else \(1 if pitch < -1e-6 else 0\)\)

        # Y from roll sign
        sy = 1 if \(1e-6 < roll < 180 - 1e-6\) else \(-1 if \(-180 + 1e-6 < roll < -1e-6\) else 0\)

        v = np.array\([float\(sx\), float\(sy\), float\(sz\)]\)
        n = np.linalg.norm\(v\)
        return v / n if n > 1e-10 else v

    n, d = check_formula\("Perfect sign decode", decode_perfect\)
    print_details\("Perfect sign decode", n, d, show_all=True\)
